title: Crispy Dutch Pomegranate Stir-fry
description: A spicy and cozy dish featuring crispy vegetables with a sweet pomegranate molasses glaze, perfect for a meal anytime.
instructions: Cut the pork belly into small cubes. Thinly slice the onion and bell peppers.
Heat a tablespoon of olive oil in a wok or large skillet over medium heat. Add the garlic and cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the sliced onions to the pan, followed by the pork belly cubes. Season with black pepper and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Next, add the diced bell peppers into the wok and continue cooking for another 2-3 minutes. The vegetables should be slightly softened but still crisp.
In a small bowl, mix pomegranate molasses with low-sodium soy sauce to create the glaze. Pour this over the pork belly mixture in the wok.
Spread sesame seeds evenly on top of the cooked pork and vegetables. Sprinkle more sesame seeds around if desired.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together nicely. Turn off heat.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course.
